items.title: GLM-5.2 leads open-weight models
items.oneliner: GLM-5.2 claims GPT-5.5-class performance with 1M context, pushing open weights closer to frontier deployment.

items.title: SubQ claims 12M-token retrieval
items.oneliner: SubQ says its 12M-token model keeps retrieval near perfect, raising the ceiling for long-context product design.

items.title: The Netherlands is building GPT-NL
items.oneliner: GPT-NL turns sovereign AI into infrastructure, not rhetoric, with transparent data governance and a national control story.

items.title: FIFA exposed World Cup streaming controls
items.oneliner: A simple role check failure exposed FIFA's World Cup streaming panel, showing how brittle high-profile access control still is.

items.title: OpenBSD carried a 27-year auth bypass
items.oneliner: OpenBSD shipped a one-line PPP auth bypass for 27 years, a reminder that mature code can hide catastrophic basics.

items.title: Chrome's ad blocker panic looks overstated
items.oneliner: A new MV3 study found no significant ad-blocking drop, so browser extension economics matter more than apocalypse headlines.

items.title: Firefox is replacing zlib with Rust
items.oneliner: Firefox 151 moves compression to zlib-rs, proving memory safety wins still demand painful low-level compatibility work.

items.title: SpaceX is buying Cursor for $60B
items.oneliner: Cursor's $60B sale makes AI coding revenue impossible to dismiss and signals brutal consolidation around developer workflows.

items.title: Datasette added SQL write support
items.oneliner: Datasette 1.0a34 adds SQL writes and full CRUD, turning a read-heavy data browser into an operational app surface.

items.title: HTTP finally gets a QUERY method
items.oneliner: RFC 10008 standardizes HTTP QUERY, giving APIs a cleaner path for safe complex queries than overloading POST.
